# `liber:session@1`

Generated reference for the `liber:session` package (`session.lsidl`). See [the LSIDL language](../../../LSIDL.md).

LiberSystem session package - shell session and job control.

## Imports

- `error` from [`liber:base@1`](/docs/gen/liber/base/v1.md)
- `task` from [`liber:process@1`](/docs/gen/liber/process/v1.md)

## Types

### record `job-info`

One tracked job in a session's job table: its small id (assigned by the session and stable across shell restarts), the program name shown by `jobs`, and whether it is currently stopped (suspended by Ctrl+Z). The live Process handle is held by the session out of band, not named here - `job-take` hands it back when a shell foregrounds the job.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `u32` |  |
| `name` | `string` |  |
| `stopped` | `bool` |  |

### record `job-entry`

A job handed back to a foregrounding shell: its info plus the live Process handle (transferred out-of-band as handle<task>), removed from the session's table by the act of taking it. The shell then drives the job in the foreground and re-registers it if it is suspended again.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `info` | `job-info` |  |
| `proc` | `handle<task>` |  |

### record `env-var`

One environment variable of a session: a name and its value. `PATH` is one such variable (seeded with a default), so a search path and user-defined variables share the same table. The shell expands `$name` references against it and persists edits in the session, so the environment survives a shell restart the way the cwd and jobs do.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `string` |  |
| `value` | `string` |  |

## Interfaces

### interface `session`

The SessionService contract: a login session is a long-lived object owning the working directory, the environment, and the job table, so the shell and the tools it runs are clients of the session rather than its owner. A shell logout or restart leaves the session - its cwd and its background jobs - intact, because SessionService, not the shell, holds them. Each client reaches its own session over a dedicated channel (the session capability the spawner mints and hands it); `cwd` reads the working directory and `chdir` moves it (the caller resolves and validates the path against the volume first, so the session is pure state and reaches no filesystem).  The job table: a shell `register`s a background or suspended job, transferring it the live Process handle (out-of-band as handle<task>); the session keeps it across shell restarts and assigns the small id. `list` enumerates the jobs, `reap` polls the running ones and returns those that have since terminated (the Process handle the session holds reads ready once the process exits), `resume` continues a stopped job in the background (the session signals it SIG_CONT), and `take` removes a job and hands its Process handle back to a shell that is foregrounding it. The session does the waiting and signalling because it, not the shell, owns the handle - so the jobs outlive any one shell.  The environment: `env-get` reads one variable, `env-set` creates or overwrites one, `env-unset` removes one, and `env-list` enumerates them all. `PATH` is seeded with a default. The shell manages these on the user's behalf (`name=value`, `env`, `unset`, `$name` expansion) and the values persist in the session across a shell restart.

Request `[op u16][corr u32][args]`, reply `[corr u32][result]`.

| op | method | signature | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | `cwd` | `cwd() -> result<string, error>` |  |
| 2 | `chdir` | `chdir(path: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 3 | `job-register` | `job-register(name: string, stopped: bool, proc: handle<task>) -> result<u32, error>` |  |
| 4 | `job-take` | `job-take(id: u32) -> result<job-entry, error>` |  |
| 5 | `job-list` | `job-list() -> result<list<job-info>, error>` |  |
| 6 | `job-reap` | `job-reap() -> result<list<job-info>, error>` |  |
| 7 | `job-resume` | `job-resume(id: u32) -> result<job-info, error>` |  |
| 8 | `env-get` | `env-get(name: string) -> result<string, error>` |  |
| 9 | `env-set` | `env-set(name: string, value: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 10 | `env-unset` | `env-unset(name: string) -> result<unit, error>` |  |
| 11 | `env-list` | `env-list() -> result<list<env-var>, error>` |  |
